item.title: 【Why Angular Performance Problems Are Often Backend Problems】为什么角度性能问题通常是后端问题
item.description:
【

Angular developers often get the blame when an app feels slow. We instinctively reach for frontend fixes optimizing components, change detection, bundle sizes, and so on. However, a significant portion of perceived Angular slowness comes not from the framework or the UI at all, but from the backend. One seasoned Angular engineer noted that most sluggish apps feel slow due to chatty APIs and oversized responses rather than the UI layer itself. In other words, you can fine tune Angular’s performance features all you want but if your API calls are slow or inefficient, the user will still be waiting on data.

The Common Misconception: The Angular App Is Slow

When performance metrics are poor, teams often assume the Angular frontend is to blame. Common first reactions include:

】<文章 data-scroll-anchor="true" data-testid="conversation-turn-12" data-turn="assistant" data-turn-id="81800770-d9e3-4b15-b7ed-60e5b2cc5178" dir="auto" tabindex="-1">

当应用程序感觉缓慢时，Angular 开发人员经常会受到指责。我们本能地寻求前端修复，优化组件、变更检测、包大小等。然而，感知到的 Angular 缓慢的很大一部分根本不是来自框架或 UI，而是来自后端。一位经验丰富的 Angular 工程师指出，大多数运行缓慢的应用程序感觉缓慢是由于繁琐的 API 和过大的响应，而不是 UI 层本身。换句话说，您可以微调 Angular 的性能功能，但如果您的 API 调用缓慢或效率低下，用户仍将等待数据。

常见误解：Angular 应用速度很慢

当性能指标不佳时，团队通常会认为 Angular 前端是罪魁祸首。常见的第一反应包括：

item.title: 【Context Lakes: The Infrastructure Layer AI Agents Need That Doesn't Exist Yet】上下文湖：人工智能代理所需的基础设施层尚不存在
item.description:
【

If you're building production AI agent systems, you've probably assembled an architecture that looks something like this: a relational database (or document store) for current state, a feature store or Redis layer for derived signals, a vector database for semantic search, and some streaming infrastructure stitching everything together.

It works. Until it doesn't.

】

如果您正在构建生产 AI 代理系统，您可能已经组装了一个如下所示的架构：用于当前状态的关系数据库（或文档存储）、用于派生信号的特征存储或 Redis 层、用于语义搜索的矢量数据库以及将所有内容缝合在一起的一些流基础设施。

它有效。直到它不再出现为止。

item.title: 【Training a Neural Network Model With Java and TensorFlow】使用 Java 和 TensorFlow 训练神经网络模型
item.description:
【

Training, exporting, and using a TensorFlow model is a great way to gain a low-level understanding of the building blocks of the LLMs fueling the AI revolution.

Since I am comfortable with using Java, I will use it to define a neural network (NN) model, train it, export it in a language-agnostic format, and then import it into a Spring Boot project. Now, doing all this from scratch would not be advisable, since there are many advances in the field of NN that would take a long time to properly understand and implementing them would be difficult and error-prone. So, to both learn about NNs and make implementation easy, we will use a proven software platform: TensorFlow.

】

训练、导出和使用 TensorFlow 模型是深入了解推动 AI 革命的法学硕士构建模块的好方法。

由于我习惯使用 Java，因此我将使用它来定义神经网络 (NN) 模型，对其进行训练，以与语言无关的格式导出，然后将其导入 Spring Boot现在，从头开始做这一切是不可取的，因为神经网络领域有许多进步，需要很长时间才能正确理解，并且实现它们会很困难且容易出错。 因此，为了了解神经网络并简化实施，我们将使用经过验证的软件平台：TensorFlow。

item.title: 【Multithreading in Modern Java: Advanced Benefits and Best Practices】现代 Java 中的多线程：高级优势和最佳实践
item.description:
【

Multithreading has always been one of core strengths of Java over years. From the early days of the JVM, Java was designed with built-in support for concurrent programming. But for many years, writing scalable multithreaded applications required careful tuning, thread pool management and constant attention to synchronization.

In the latest Java versions, the concurrency model has evolved significantly. Modern Java introduces improvements such as Virtual Threads, better executors, improved fork-join performance and more structured concurrency approaches. These features allow developers to build highly concurrent applications with simpler code and fewer scalability limitations.

】

多线程多年来一直是 Java 的核心优势之一。从 JVM 的早期开始，Java 就被设计为内置对并发编程的支持。但多年来，编写可扩展的多线程应用程序需要仔细调整、线程池管理和对同步的持续关注。

在最新的 Java 版本中，并发模型发生了显着的变化。现代 Java 引入了一些改进，例如虚拟线程、更好的执行器、改进的 fork-join 性能和更结构化的并发方法。这些功能使开发人员能够使用更简单的代码和更少的可扩展性限制来构建高度并发的应用程序。

item.title: 【Fine-Tuning of Spring Cache】Spring缓存的微调
item.description:
【

Caching is one of the most effective techniques for improving the performance of modern Spring applications. Especially in microservice architectures or high-traffic APIs, a well-configured cache can significantly reduce database load and greatly improve response times. By storing frequently accessed data in memory, applications can avoid repeated expensive operations such as database queries or external API calls. This article provides a compact yet comprehensive overview of Spring’s caching capabilities. 

For example, without caching, each request follows this process:

】

缓存是提高现代 Spring 应用程序性能的最有效技术之一。尤其是在微服务架构或高流量 API 中，配置良好的缓存可以显着减少数据库负载并极大缩短响应时间。通过将频繁访问的数据存储在内存中，应用程序可以避免重复的昂贵操作，例如数据库查询或外部 API 调用。本文对 Spring 的缓存功能进行了紧凑而全面的概述。 

例如，在没有缓存的情况下，每个请求都遵循以下流程：

item.title: 【AI-Powered Dev Workflows: How SWEs Are Shipping Faster in 2026】AI 驱动的开发工作流程：SWE 如何在 2026 年加快交付速度
item.description:
【

By 2026, the role of the Software Engineer (SWE) has shifted from manual code authorship to high-level system orchestration. The integration of large language models (LLMs) and specialized AI agents into every stage of the software development lifecycle (SDLC) has enabled teams to achieve 10x delivery speeds. However, shipping faster is only half the battle; shipping with quality and security remains the priority.

This guide outlines the industry-standard best practices for navigating AI-powered development workflows, focusing on context management, prompt engineering, and autonomous testing.

】

到 2026 年，软件工程师 (SWE) 的角色已从手动代码编写转变为高级系统编排。将大型语言模型 (LLM) 和专门的AI 代理集成到软件开发生命周期 (SDLC) 的每个阶段使团队能够实现 10 倍的交付速度。然而，更快的运输只是成功的一半。质量和安全的运输仍然是首要任务。

本指南概述了用于导航人工智能驱动的开发工作流程的行业标准最佳实践，重点关注上下文管理、即时工程和自主测试。

item.title: 【Velocity Is Not Enough: Rethinking Risk in Agile Software Development】速度还不够：重新思考敏捷软件开发中的风险
item.description:
【

Agile has transformed software delivery by optimizing for speed, adaptability, and customer value. Teams track velocity, monitor burndown charts, and celebrate incremental releases. But in complex high-reliability software systems, velocity alone is not a meaningful success metric. A sprint can close on time. Features can ship. Story points can burn down. And risk can still increase.

The fundamental issue is not that Agile ignores risk — it’s that it often treats risk as a vague concept. In reality, software programs operate across multiple distinct risk dimensions, each requiring different mitigation strategies and visibility.

】

敏捷通过优化速度、适应性和客户价值改变了软件交付。团队跟踪速度、监控燃尽图并庆祝增量发布。但在复杂的高可靠性软件系统中，速度本身并不是一个有意义的成功指标。冲刺可以按时结束。功能可以发货。故事点可能会被烧毁。而且风险仍然会增加。

根本问题不在于敏捷忽视风险，而是它经常将风险视为一个模糊的概念。实际上，软件程序在多个不同的风险维度上运行，每个维度都需要不同的缓解策略和可见性。

item.title: 【Part II: The Network That Doesn't Exist: Zero Trust, Service Meshes, and the Slow Death of Perimeter Security】第二部分：不存在的网络：零信任、服务网格和外围安全的缓慢消亡
item.description:
【

The conversation that reordered my understanding of enterprise network security happened in a conference room in London in early 2019. The CISO of a mid-size financial services firm — precise, methodical, someone whose threat modeling I trusted — was describing her organization's response to a pen test finding. The testers had gotten onto one internal server through a phishing email. From that single initial access point, within seventy-two hours, they had lateral movement access to fourteen other systems, including two that handled customer account data.

The perimeter had been intact throughout. The firewall logs showed nothing anomalous crossing the network boundary. Everything that happened after the initial email was internal traffic, authenticated by the fact that it came from inside the network. There was no enforcement, no verification, nothing that asked whether this particular server had any business talking to those other fourteen.

】

2019 年初，在伦敦的一间会议室里进行的对话重新整理了我对企业网络安全的理解。一家中型金融服务公司的 CISO 精确、有条理，我信任他的威胁建模，他正在描述她的组织对渗透测试结果的响应。测试人员通过网络钓鱼电子邮件进入了一台内部服务器。从这个单一的初始访问点，在七十二小时内，他们可以横向移动访问其他十四个系统，其中包括两个处理客户帐户数据的系统。

整个周边都完好无损。防火墙日志显示没有任何跨越网络边界的异常情况。最初的电子邮件之后发生的所有事情都是内部流量，通过它来自网络内部的事实进行验证。没有强制执行，没有验证，也没有询问该特定服务器是否有任何业务与其他十四个服务器交谈。

item.title: 【Part I: The Build You Can’t See Is the One That Will Kill You: Software Supply Chains, SBOMs, and the Long Reckoning After SolarWinds】第一部分：你看不到的构建将会杀死你：软件供应链、SBOM 以及 SolarWinds 后的长期清算
item.description:
【

There is a specific quality of dread that experienced security practitioners get when they think carefully about what happened in December 2020. Not the dread of a novel attack technique, or an adversary with exceptional resources. The dread of recognizing, in granular detail, exactly how many organizations were equally exposed and simply weren't targeted. The SolarWinds compromise — where a trojanized software update was distributed through a vendor's legitimate build pipeline and installed with full trust by thousands of downstream customers — was not primarily a story about sophisticated tradecraft. It was a story about the industry's collective decision to trust software artifacts it couldn't inspect, from processes it couldn't verify, at a scale that made the assumption catastrophically fragile.

Four years later, I want to report something encouraging: the reckoning has started. I want to be careful about how encouraging I make it sound, because the progress is real but the baseline was so poor that real progress still leaves us badly positioned.

】

当经验丰富的安全从业者仔细思考 2020 年 12 月发生的事情时，他们会感到一种特定的恐惧。不是对新颖的攻击技术的恐惧，也不是对拥有特殊资源的对手的恐惧。害怕详细地认识到到底有多少组织同样受到暴露，但根本没有成为目标。 SolarWinds 妥协——木马软件更新通过供应商的合法构建管道分发，并得到数千名下游客户的完全信任安装——主要并不是一个关于复杂技术的故事。这是一个关于行业集体决定信任它无法检查的软件工件、来自它无法验证的流程的故事，其规模使得这一假设变得灾难性地脆弱。

四年后，我想报告一些令人鼓舞的事情：清算已经开始。我想谨慎对待我所说的如何令人鼓舞，因为进步是真实的，但基线太差，以至于真正的进步仍然让我们处于不利地位。

item.title: 【Advanced Auto Loader Patterns for Large-Scale JSON and Semi-Structured Data】适用于大规模 JSON 和半结构化数据的高级自动加载器模式
item.description:
【

Databricks Auto Loader is a managed feature of Spark that incrementally and efficiently processes new data files as they arrive in cloud storage. It supports JSON and many semi-structured formats and is widely used to handle large-scale ingestion of flexible schemas.


Auto Loader incrementally pulls new JSON or other files from cloud storage and writes them to Delta Lake tables for downstream analytics.

】

Databricks Auto Loader 是 Spark 的一项托管功能，可在新数据文件到达云存储时以增量方式高效地对其进行处理。它支持 JSON 和许多半结构化格式，并广泛用于处理灵活模式的大规模摄取。


Auto加载器从云存储中增量提取新的 JSON 或其他文件，并将它们写入 Delta Lake 表以进行下游分析。
